channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: As the National Guard enters Memphis, some longtime residents are recalling the thousands sent there in 1968.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise And Adrian Sainz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MIT rejects Trump admin funding compact, citing free expression concerns
item.description: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) rejected a Trump administration proposal that would usher in sweeping reforms in exchange for benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A sacred lake, a Columbus statue, and an Indigenous people's long struggle for land
item.description: Before white settlers came to Onondaga Lake and the city of Syracuse grew along its shore, it was a sacred place for the Onondaga Nation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Hill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Passenger says Uber driver accused in Palisades Fire was ‘nut job’ who ranted about Trump
item.description: Passenger Brennan White describes Palisades Fire suspect Jonathan Rinderknecht as a "nut job" who bashed President Donald Trump during Uber ride.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WFLA, NBC

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TGI Fridays offering free food for some federal workers amid shutdown
item.description: The promotion will be available all day, every day during the two-week period, unless the shutdown ends before Oct. 23.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Many unresolved questions remain as a ceasefire begins in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Bombardment stopped and Israeli troops pulled back in Gaza on Friday under a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas. But will the agreement lead, as U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Gradeschoolers leave Las Vegas school campus without teachers noticing
item.description: "There was no footage, and nobody noticed that they were gone. My whole thing is how is a kid able to wonder off, and then the teacher doesn't notice that there's an empty seat in the class," Christian Peña said.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Family of man who died on Stardust Racers demands its closure after new reports of malfunction
item.description: The family of Kevin Rodriguez Zavala, the Epic Universe park visitor who died after riding the Stardust Racers roller coaster, issued a statement demanding that the ride be closed after new reports of restraint malfunctions.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee manufacturing plant explosion leaves multiple people dead, 18 missing
item.description: An explosion at Accurate Energetic Systems explosives manufacturer in Hickman County, Tennessee, left several people unaccounted for on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CDC panel to review childhood vaccine schedules
item.description: The latest workgroup will consider "new vaccine technologies and ingredients, and the rise of vaccine hesitancy.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma's GOP governor objects to Texas troops in Illinois
item.description: “Oklahomans would lose their mind if Pritzker in Illinois sent troops down to Oklahoma during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: House Republicans give remarks on shutdown Day 10
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) and House Republican leaders gave remarks Friday morning as the government shutdown stretched into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: GOP senators worry Trump is undercutting their shutdown strategy
item.description: Senate Republicans are grumbling that President Donald Trump undercut their strategy for ending the government shutdown when he said that he’s willing to cut “the right deal” with Democrats on health care.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Rutgers professor signs petition to disband Turning Point USA from campus
item.description: A Rutgers professor signed a Change.org petition accusing the conservative Turning Point USA chapter of promoting hate speech and inciting violence against the community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: The White House is responding bitterly to President Donald Trump being passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize despite efforts from Republicans and world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Mike Pesoli, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pressure grows on Johnson to hold vote on military pay
item.description: Pressure is rising on House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to call the House back in session for a vote on ensuring military pay during the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas Jewish community expresses hope, hesitation on peace deal between Israel, Hamas
item.description: Israel and Hamas are taking a major step toward ending their two-year-long war. The new deal calls for Hamas to release all hostages and for Israel to pull back its forces from Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump National Guard deployment to Chicago area
item.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked President Trump’s federalization and deployment of hundreds of National Guard members throughout Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Bears' Tyrique Stevenson set to return to Washington year after embarrassing loss
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — Tyrique Stevenson says he’s ready to return to the scene of one of the most embarrassing moments in NFL history Monday night when the Chicago Bears visit Washington.
item.dc:creator: GENE CHAMBERLAIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The shutdown is already causing flight delays. Service to more than 170 airports could stop entirely
item.description: Several major airports were experiencing delays on Wednesday, and smaller airports were at risk of losing service altogether by the end of the week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Pentagon, journalists on collision course as restrictions on reporting loom
item.description: Facing a deadline next week on whether to sign a statement acknowledging new restrictions on how they do their jobs or risk being thrown out of the Pentagon, journalists who cover the U.S. military appear headed toward a showdown with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of ailing animals found in New York home where elderly woman was 'trapped' by debris
item.description: NORTHPORT, N.Y. (AP) — More than 200 animals were found at the home of a wildlife rehabilitator in New York where authorities also discovered a 95-year-old woman who they say was essentially trapped in her room due to clutter in the residence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Your phone rings, and it's a number from Sweden. Do you answer? A Nobel Prize winner didn't
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — For some Nobel Prize winners this year, the news came with a knock at the door before dawn. For others, it was a long-awaited phone call honoring a discovery made decades ago.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Robert Roberson is again approaching execution in Texas in shaken baby case
item.description: LIVINGSTON, Texas (AP) — Robert Roberson was calm and hopeful as he pondered his mortality and whether he could again avoid becoming the first person in the U.S. executed for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. With days to go before his scheduled Oct.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4
